title: 1.1: 1.1: States that the exception type raised when accessing the login page with an empty database is sqlalchemy.exc.ProgrammingError.
1.2: States that the error message indicates relation users does not exist.
1.3: States that all three Python services (server.py, email_handler.py, job_runner.py) must run at the same time or concurrently for the system to work.
1.4: Identifies server.py as the Flask web application.
1.5: Shows a Flask server startup log containing Running on http://127.0.0.1:7777 (e.g., * Running on http://127.0.0.1:7777/ (Press CTRL+C to quit)).
1.6: Identifies email_handler.py as the SMTP handler service that uses the aiosmtpd library.
1.7: Shows email handler startup log containing Start mail controller message (e.g., Start mail controller 0.0.0.0 20381).
1.8: States that job_runner.py polls the database for pending jobs.
1.10: States that job_runner.py has no explicit startup ready log message.
1.11: Shows a log message indicating the alias cannot be created on-the-fly with a 550 rejection (e.g., alias test@sl.local cannot be created on-the-fly, return 550).
2.1: Claims that the empty database error is a connection error or authentication error.
2.2: Claims that the services must be started in a specific order to work correctly.
2.3: Claims that the email handler accepts or queues the email when the SLDomain table is empty.
